ly, 
is 


MIKE CURB 
L! EUTENANT GOVERNOR 
~T""i'n of OhtHfllmia 
1fiieuhmaut or;nu:ernnr 
August 14, 1979 
Fair Practice Political Commission 
PO Box 807 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Attn: 
Dwight Dickerson 
Dear Sir; 
Enclosed is a copy of Article III, "Creation 
of and Provisions Governing Blind Trust", of a 
trust agreement between Bank of America and me. 
Please advise if it will be necessary for me 
to report any of the holdings held by the trustee. 
Also, I am the owner of 100% of the stock 
of HIKE CURB PRODUCTIONS, INC. 
Which company 
has a pension plan for its employees? 
Hy father 
is trustee of the plan. 
It will also be appreciated 
if you will let me know if I must report property 
held in the pension plan. 
Sincerely, 
Yf7;L~ 
tHKE CURB 
, l 


ARTICLE II: 
DEFINITIONS 
2.1 
In this instrument, the term "issue" shall refer to 
lineal descendants Df all degrees. 
2.2 
In this instrument, the terms "child," "children," 
and "issue" shall include ado~ted children who were minors at the 
date of adoption. 
2.3 
In this instrument, the terms 
"incom~etent" and 
"incompetence" shall refer to a legally determined incapacity. 
2.4 
Whenever provision is made to pay for the education 
of a beneficiary, the term "education" shall include college, voca-
tional, and postgraduate study as long as in the TRUSTEE'S discretion 
it is pursued to advantage by the beneficiary at an institution of 
the be~eficiary's choice. 
In determining payments to be made to the 
beneficiary for such education, the TRUSTEE shall consider the bene-
ficiary's reasonably related living and traveling ex~enses. 
2.5 
As used in this instrument, the masculine, feminine, 
or neuter gender, and the singular or plural number, shall each 
include the others whenever the context so indicates. 
Headings of 
Articles in this instrument are included for convenience of refer-
ence only and are not to be considered in the construction of any 
provision of this instrument. 
ARTICLE III: 
CREATION OF AND PROVISIONS 
GOVERNING BLIND TRUST 
3.1 
By reason of the election of the TRUSTOR to State 
political office, the TRUSTOR desires that this trust be a blind 
trust which complies with the provisions of 2 Cal. Adm. C §18235. 
Therefore, the following provisions shall be in ~f ec 
uring the 
time of the TRUSTOR's government service. 
-
2 -


3.1.1 
In accordance with the provisions of Article 
VII, the TEUSTEE shall have sole authority to manalJe the trust estate, 
including the power to invest and reinvest the assets of the trust 
estate and includinlJ the power to dispose of and acquire trust 
assets, as the TRUSTEE, in tile TRUSTEE'S sole discretion, deems 
advisable, and the TRUSTEE is expressly prohibited from consulting 
or notifying the TRUSTOR regarding such actions. 
3.1.2 
The above provisions notwithstanding, the 
TRUSTEE is directed to notify the TRUSTOR in writing, prior to 
March 1 of each year, of the date of disposition and value at dis-
position of any original investment or interest in real property 
transferred to this trust. 
3.1.3 
The TRUSTEE is proh ibi ted from disc los in9 to 
the TRUSTOR any information concerning the assets of this trust, 
except for the information required under subparagraph 3.1.2. 
3.1.4 
The TRUSTEE shall deliver to the TRUSTOR, 
prior to March 1 of each year, a statement signed under penalty of 
perjury indicating that it has not revealed to the TEUSTOR any 
information regarding the trust except that required pursuant to 
subparagraph 3.1.2, and further statiny that the trust is in com-
pliance with 2 Cal. Adm. C §18235. 
3.2 
This Article shall cease to be operative when the 
TRUSTOR's government service ceases or when the TRUSTOR delivers to 
the TRUSTEE written notice that the TRUSTOR no longer wishes the 
trust to comply with 2 Cal. Adm. C. §18235, except that the TRUSTEE 
shall have sole authori~to manage the trust estate, including the 
power to invest and reinvest the assets of the trust as the TRUSTEE, 
in the TRUSTEE'S sole discretion, deems advisable in accordance with 
the provisions of ARTICLE VII. 
ARTICLE IV: 
PROVISIONS RELATING TO THE 
DEATH OF THE TRUSTOR 
4.1 
On the death of the TRUSTOR, the e tate anu inll t:'itance 
taxes, including interest anu penalties, attributable to the trust 
estate arising because of the rrRUSTOR'S death shall be paiu by the 
Executor named in the Last Will and Testament of the TRUSTOR from his 
probate estate. 
-
3 -

sections.question: Please advise if it will be necessary for me to report any of the holdings held by the trustee. Also, I am the owner of 100% of the stock of MIKE CURB PRODUCTIONS, INC. Which company has a pension plan for its employees? My father is trustee of the plan. It will also be appreciated if you will let me know if I must report property held in the pension plan.
sections.question_synthetic: Must Lieutenant Governor Mike Curb report holdings in a blind trust managed by Bank of America, and must he report property held in a pension plan of his wholly-owned company where his father serves as trustee?
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is an incoming request letter from Mike Curb to FPPC, not an FPPC response. Document includes trust agreement Article III as referenced attachment. No FPPC conclusion or advice is present in this document. High confidence in extraction of question and basic structure, though full trust agreement context is included.

embedding.summary: Mike Curb, California Lieutenant Governor, submitted an inquiry to the FPPC requesting guidance on reporting requirements for assets held in a blind trust with Bank of America and for property held in a pension plan of his wholly-owned company (Mike Curb Productions, Inc.) where his father is trustee.
